div.head: The Secretary of State to the Chargé in Germany (Mayer)
div.p: 98. Your 237, July 30, 6 p.m. While I naturally would not wish to interfere with any arrangements that are being made on Simpson’s behalf or in his interest, the fact remains that I have presented this matter to the German Ambassador here, who has no doubt already reported to his Government. It was my understanding that the court had appointed a lawyer to defend Simpson, and that this lawyer was actively engaged in preparing Simpson’s defense. I do feel that the representations in question should not be unduly delayed.
